titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Lesbian, Gay, Bisexual, and Transgender Families Project, Yale Research Initiative on the History of Sexualities, Records
Lesbian, Gay, Bisexual, and Transgender Families Project, Yale Research Initiative on the History of Sexualities, Records
titlestmt.author: compiled by Mary Caldera

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday November 9, 2018 at 10:15

did.unittitle: Lesbian, Gay, Bisexual, and Transgender Families Project, Yale Research Initiative on the History of Sexualities, records
did.abstract: The records consist of oral history interviews, reports, a timeline, and a small collection of printed materials documenting the history of the lesbian, gay, bisexual, and transgender (LGBT) family equality movement in the United States. Most of the individuals interviewed were associated with the following organizations: All Our Families Coalition; Center Families (New York City); Children of Lesbians and Gays Everywhere (COLAGE); Family Equality Council (Boston) and its predecessors (Gay Fathers Coalition and Gay and Lesbian Parents Coalition International); Fenway Health (Boston); Gay and Lesbian Adolescent Social Services (GLASS); Our Family Coalition (San Francisco Bay Area); and Rainbow Families (Minneapolis). Additional interviewees include attorneys and mental health and fertility services providers.

corpname.part: Yale Research Initiative on the History of Sexuality

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: The Lesbian, Gay, Bisexual, and Transgender (LGBT) Families Project was sponsored by the Yale Research Initiative on the History of Sexualities. The project was conducted by Emily Johnson and Joey Plaster, Yale University doctoral candidates, under the supervision of George Chauncey, Samuel Knight Professor of History and American Studies.

The goal of the project was to document the history of the LGBT family equality movement in the United States and focused on a few of the major support, advocacy and service organizations for gay, lesbian, bisexual, and transgender families. The project ran from summer 2012 to spring 2013.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Transferred by the Yale Research Initiative on the History of Sexualities, 2012-2013.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The records consist of oral history interviews, reports, a timeline, and a small collection of printed materials documenting the history of the lesbian, gay, bisexual, and transgender (LGBT) family equality movement in the United States. Most of the individuals interviewed were associated with the following organizations: All Our Families Coalition; Center Families (New York City); Children of Lesbians and Gays Everywhere (COLAGE); Family Equality Council (Boston) and its predecessors (Gay Fathers Coalition and Gay and Lesbian Parents Coalition International); Fenway Health (Boston); Gay and Lesbian Adolescent Social Services (GLASS); Our Family Coalition (San Francisco Bay Area); and Rainbow Families (Minneapolis). Additional interviewees include attorneys and mental health and fertility services providers.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The records are aranged in two series: I. Oral history interviews, 2012-2013. II. Reports and historical documents, circa 1990-1912.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Lesbian, Gay, Bisexual, and Transgender Families Project, Yale Research Initiative on the History of Sexualities, Records (RU 1124). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright for materials authored or otherwise produced as official business of Yale University is retained by Yale University. Unless otherwise noted in the Collection Contents, copyright for the interviews is held by Yale University. Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Some restrictions apply. Details provided at the file level.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by last name of interviewee.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Davenport was a member of Gay and Lesbian Parents Coalition International (later Family Equality Council) and originator of Family Week, an lgbt family event in Provincetown, Massachusettes.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of brief research reports on organizations and individuals relevant to the history of lesbian, gay, bisexual, and transgender family movement in the United States, which were compiled by Emily Johnson and Joey Plaster in preparation for conducting the oral history interviews. Also included are a spreadsheet with interview information (duplicated in the collection inventory) and a timeline: "Brief History of GLBT Parenting, with focus on the U.S."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Consists of reports, articles, forms and survey responses given to the Emily Johnson for the archive, by a Gerry Tyrrell and a member of the Fenway Community Health.
